summary: Removed the redundant merge queue wrapper workflow so `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is the single authoritative `merge_group` entry point while preserving pull request and push-to-main CI triggers.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: There is one authoritative merge-queue entry point.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Removed `.github/workflows/merge-queue.yml`; `.github/workflows/ci.yml` remains the only workflow with a `merge_group` trigger.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Pull request and push-to-main CI behavior remains unchanged.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: No changes were made to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`; it still declares `pull_request` and `push` with `branches: [main]`.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Merge queue coverage remains present via `merge_group`.
Evidence records workflow lint and repo hygiene checks.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `commands.log` records `nix develop -c actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml`, `nix develop -c just check`, and `git diff --check`.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No product runtime, capture, encode, transport, decode, render, presentation, cross-device, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `evidence/m4-merge-group-trigger-dedupe/notes.md` records the workflow-routing-only boundary and no product claims were added.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node only changes GitHub Actions workflow routing and evidence.
